relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The community is reminded to be ready for every beautiful work.
No slander, no quarrel, but gentleness toward all humans.
The old pattern was foolishness, envy, and mutual hatred.
Saving/healing comes by mercy, washing, rebirth, and Spirit renewal.
The Spirit is poured richly through Jesus Messiah.
Grace makes heirs in hope of age-long life.
Trust is careful to stand before beautiful works.
Empty quarrels and speculations are avoided.
Divisiveness is corrected, then refused if it persists.
Titus is invited to join Paul at Nicopolis.
Zenas and Apollos are to be sent with care.
The people learn fruitful works for necessary needs.
The letter closes with love in trust and grace for all.

contemplative_note: Titus 3 is mercy becoming social practice: kindness appears, Spirit renews, quarrels are refused, and beautiful works meet real needs.
